commit.message:
Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME directly instead of dirs::config_dir() (#284)

* Initial plan

* Fix XDG directory table in docs to match dirs v6 behavior

The documentation table showed stale macOS and Windows paths from
dirs v5. With dirs v6:
- macOS config_dir() returns ~/Library/Application Support (not
  ~/Library/Preferences)
- Windows config_dir() returns %APPDATA% (not %LOCALAPPDATA%)
- state_dir() returns None on macOS and Windows (falls back to
  data directory)

This made the config file appear to be in $XDG_STATE_HOME territory
on macOS, when it was actually in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME all along.

Fixes #283

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME directly instead of dirs::config_dir()

Replace all 4 uses of `dirs::config_dir()` with a new `xdg_config_home()`
helper that reads `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (falling back to `$HOME/.config`).
This follows the XDG Base Directory spec directly rather than using the
platform-specific mappings from the `dirs` crate, which on macOS returns
`~/Library/Application Support` instead of `~/.config`.

The documentation table in `docs/configuration.md` is simplified to show
only the XDG defaults since the paths are now consistent across platforms.

Fixes #283

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: improve xdg_config_home docs and test names

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use %APPDATA% as config fallback on Windows instead of ~/.config

On Windows, fall back to %APPDATA% (the conventional roaming config
directory) when $XDG_CONFIG_HOME is not set. On Unix/macOS the fallback
remains $HOME/.config. The XDG env var is always respected on every
platform when explicitly set.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract shared home_dot_config() helper and improve Windows test

Address code review: deduplicate the $HOME/.config fallback into a shared
helper function, and use expect() in the Windows test so it fails
explicitly when %APPDATA% is unexpectedly missing.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: return Option from xdg_config_home, validate XDG values, parameterized tests

- xdg_config_home() now returns Option<PathBuf> (None when no config dir
  can be determined), fixing the relative-path fallback issue
- Ignores empty and non-absolute $XDG_CONFIG_HOME values per XDG spec
- Extracted pure resolve_config_home() helper for deterministic testing
- Updated all 4 call sites to propagate the Option properly
- config_path_from/components_path_from now return Option<PathBuf>
- Store::open and StateInfo::new use .context() for clear error messages
- 8 deterministic unit tests covering all resolve_config_home branches

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix spelling: Honour -> Honor for codebase consistency

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows CI: use platform-appropriate absolute paths in xdg tests

Unix-style paths like /custom/config are not absolute on Windows
(they lack a drive letter). Add an abs() test helper that prefixes
C: on Windows so all resolve_config_home tests work cross-platform.

Wire up sync server to mywebnicek app

Rewrite packages/react/sync.ts to use the actual sync server protocol
(type: 'sync' with frontiers tracking, /sync?room= URL format) instead
of the incompatible join/events protocol.

App.tsx now auto-connects to the Azure sync server on mount, with:
- Room ID from URL hash (shareable links)
- Sync status indicator in the header bar
- Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff
- 1-second auto-sync polling

Also set Azure Container App minReplicas=1 to keep the sync server
always available for WebSocket connections.

feat: implement HMM regime detection with Monte Carlo scenario generation

Replace the simple log-normal p5/p95 approach with a 2-state Gaussian
Hidden Markov Model that detects bull/bear market regimes and generates
scenarios via regime-conditioned Monte Carlo simulation.

New algorithm (src/utils/hmm.ts):
- Baum-Welch EM with 5 random restarts for robust fitting
- Forward-backward algorithm for regime detection
- 3000-path Monte Carlo GBM with Markov chain regime transitions
- Seeded PRNG (Mulberry32) for deterministic results
- Graceful fallback to original log-normal model on convergence failure

UI updates:
- ScenarioEditor shows HMM regime badge (bull/bear) in analysis header
- Methodology description updated with HMM explanation
- Per-state stats (mu, sigma, expected duration) shown in expanded panel

Also translates bull-bear-scenarios.md skill from Polish to English,
removes citation artifacts, adds globs and Njord implementation notes.

commit.message:
Fix bad-string-token to return null from get_token_value() (#226)

Per the [CSS tokenization
spec](https://www.w3.org/TR/css-syntax-3/#tokenization),
`bad-string-token` carries no associated data.
`CSSProcessor::get_token_value()` was incorrectly returning the partial
string content up to the breaking codepoint instead of `null`.

Fixes #225.

## Changes

- **`CSS/class-cssprocessor.php`**: Splits `TOKEN_BAD_STRING` out of the
shared `TOKEN_STRING`/`TOKEN_URL` case in `get_token_value()`, giving it
a dedicated case that returns `null`. Also removes a no-op
self-assignment (`$this->token_value = $this->token_value`).
- **`Tests/CSSProcessorTest.php`**: Adds
`test_bad_string_token_value_is_null()` covering the exact scenario from
the issue report.

```php
$p = CSSProcessor::create( "'str\ning'" );
$p->next_token();
// Before: returned 'str' — After: returns null
assert( $p->get_token_type() === CSSProcessor::TOKEN_BAD_STRING );
assert( $p->get_token_value() === null );
```

commit.message:
feat: Storage Inspector (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, IndexedDB)

Adds full read/write/clear access to all browser storage areas for the
current tab  useful for seeding test state, inspecting authorigin
tokens, and resetting app state between test runs.

Types (packages/shared/src/browser.ts):
  - StorageArea ("local" | "session")
  - StorageEntry { key, value, bytes }
  - CookieEntry { name, value, domain, path, expires, httpOnly, secure, sameSite, size }
  - IndexedDbObjectStore { name, keyPath, autoIncrement, count, rows[] }
  - IndexedDbDatabase { name, version, objectStores }
  - StorageReport { localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, indexedDb, totalBytes }

TabManager (apps/desktop/src/main/tab-manager.ts):
  - captureStorage(): full snapshot via Runtime.evaluate + Network.getCookies
  - getStorage(area, key?): read all or single entry from local/session
  - setStorage(area, entries): batch write to local/session storage
  - clearStorage(area, keys?): remove specific keys or clear entire area
  - setCookie(): upsert via Network.setCookie
  - deleteCookie(name, url?): remove by name
  - clearCookies(url?): delete all cookies for the origin

REST (apps/desktop/src/main/agent-server.ts):
  - GET/POST/DELETE /api/tabs/:id/storage, /storage/local, /storage/session
  - GET/POST/DELETE /api/tabs/:id/cookies, /cookies/:name

SDK (packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts):
  - captureStorage, getStorage, setStorage, clearStorage
  - getCookies, setCookie, deleteCookie, clearCookies
  - delete() helper now accepts optional body
  - Re-exports all new types

Docs (docs/dev-team-features.md):
  - Section 5 fully documented with full API table, shape definitions,
    and usage examples

EXP-1261-1270: optimized production stack and deep diagnostics

Key findings:
- EXP-1261: Optimized full stack (d2/300t/lr0.03+T+Q+MH) = +0.033, 9/11 wins
  Patient h improves from ~0 to 0.146 (+0.146)
- EXP-1262: 30-min full stack R²=0.756, +0.016, 10/11 wins
  Clinically useful: approaching CGM-grade at forward prediction
- EXP-1267: Uniform averaging BEATS Ridge stacking (0.491 vs 0.470)
  Simplicity wins — Ridge overfits validation set
- EXP-1270: Clinical validation: MARD=9.1%, Zone A=88.7%
  Best patient k: MARD=5.2%, Zone A=98.0%
- EXP-1265: AR correction = exactly zero at single-model level
  Confirms EXP-1211 0.781 requires multi-scale ensemble mechanism
- EXP-1266: Error scales 50% higher at >250 mg/dL vs normal range
  Rising/stable/post-meal patterns have similar RMSE
- EXP-1263: LOO transfer hurts (-0.004) — selective > indiscriminate
- EXP-1264: Feature pruning negligible — top 2 features = 19% importance
- EXP-1268/1269: Monotonic constraints and interactions = zero impact

fix: add root vercel.json for monorepo safety + document Vercel incident

Root package.json was added for Railway/Railpack but Vercel had no matching
root-level config. When Vercel points at the repo root (not frontend/), it
cannot detect Next.js or find the .next output directory.

Fix:
- Add root vercel.json with explicit framework, installCommand, buildCommand,
  outputDirectory, and security headers for the repo-root deploy case
- frontend/vercel.json remains the preferred path when Vercel dashboard is
  set to Root Directory = frontend

Also document both Vercel incidents in GUIDE.md troubleshooting section.

Note: the Linux native package fix (lightningcss + @tailwindcss/oxide) from
earlier commits also fixes Vercel's Linux build environment.

Manual step: in Vercel dashboard -> General -> Root Directory -> set to 'frontend'
for the cleanest deployment path.

Fix atomic attempt upsert and reliable missing-attempt detection in step operations (#85)

Three correctness issues in the MongoDB step/attempt upsert layer:
`ensureAttemptExists` had a read-then-write race that could produce
duplicate attempts; `UpsertStepEnd` used `ModifiedCount==0` as a "not
found" error which fires spuriously on idempotent updates; and
`upsertStepInTestAttempt` used the same unreliable `ModifiedCount==0`
signal to detect missing attempts (masked by `$set: {"updated_at": now}`
always modifying the matched document).

## `ensureAttemptExists` — atomic pipeline replaces read-then-write

Replaced `$not: {$elemMatch}` + `$push` with a single aggregation
pipeline update that conditionally appends only when `retry_index` is
absent:

```go
update := bson.A{bson.D{{Key: "$set", Value: bson.M{
    "tests": bson.M{"$map": bson.M{
        "input": "$tests", "as": "test",
        "in": bson.M{"$cond": bson.A{
            bson.M{"$eq": bson.A{"$$test.id", testID}},
            bson.M{"$mergeObjects": bson.A{"$$test", bson.M{
                "attempts": bson.M{"$cond": bson.A{
                    bson.M{"$in": bson.A{retryIndex, existingRetryIndexesExpr}},
                    bson.M{"$ifNull": bson.A{"$$test.attempts", bson.A{}}},
                    bson.M{"$concatArrays": bson.A{...existing..., bson.A{attempt}}},
                }},
            }}},
            "$$test",
        }},
    }},
}}}}
```

## `upsertStepInTestAttempt` — tight filter + `MatchedCount` as signal

The filter now includes `$elemMatch` down to the attempt, so
`MatchedCount==0` reliably means "attempt absent" regardless of the
`$set` side-effect:

```go
filter := bson.M{"_id": runID, "tests": bson.M{"$elemMatch": bson.M{
    "id": testID,
    "attempts": bson.M{"$elemMatch": bson.M{"retry_index": retry_index}},
}}}
```

`MatchedCount==0` → call `ensureAttemptExists` + retry; second
`MatchedCount==0` → return error.

## `UpsertStepEnd` — `MatchedCount` over `ModifiedCount`

Filter tightened to `$elemMatch` all the way to `steps.id`.
`MatchedCount==0` = step not found (hard error). `ModifiedCount==0` =
idempotent no-op (warn only).

## Tests

Added `mongodb_step_begin_test.go` with three integration tests
covering:
- Empty `attempts: []` triggers `ensureAttemptExists` and inserts the
step
- Repeated `ensureAttemptExists` calls are idempotent (no duplicate
attempts)
- Realistic retry scenario: attempt[0] exists, step for attempt[1]
creates attempt[1] without affecting attempt[0]

---------

fix: use context.attach/detach instead of baggage.attach/detach in auth middleware (#217)

The opentelemetry.baggage module has no attach/detach methods.
These belong to opentelemetry.context. baggage.set_baggage() returns
a Context object that must be activated via context.attach(ctx).
